title: Skytron 6500 Series - Table Will Not Power On
description: Guide for Clinical Engineering to troubleshoot a Skytron 6500 Series surgical table that will not power on.

steps.title: Verify Power Source
steps.instructions: Ensure the table is plugged into a known working outlet. Test the outlet with a voltmeter or another device. Reason: A dead or tripped outlet is a common, easily fixable cause.

steps.title: Check Circuit Breakers and Fuses
steps.instructions: Inspect wall or table circuit breakers. Reset if tripped. Check table-specific fuses if accessible externally. Reason: Overcurrent protection may prevent the table from powering.

steps.title: Inspect Power Cord
steps.instructions: Look for cuts, fraying, or loose connections at both ends. Swap with a known good power cord if available. Reason: Damaged cords can prevent power without visible signs on the table.

steps.title: Examine Main Power Switch
steps.instructions: Ensure the main power switch on the table base is turned on. Toggle it off and on again. Reason: Sometimes the switch may not fully engage.

steps.title: Check Battery Backup (If Equipped)
steps.instructions: Some Skytron 6500 tables have internal batteries for limited operation. Confirm battery is seated and charged, or bypass if external AC power is available. Reason: A dead backup battery may prevent control electronics from booting.

steps.title: Observe Indicator Lights
steps.instructions: Look for any LEDs on the table base or pendant. No lights often indicate power not reaching internal electronics.

CCR.Complaint: Table will not power on; no lights or response from controls.
CCR.Cause: Verified outlet functional, power cord intact, circuit breakers reset; table remained unresponsive.
CCR.Resolution: Table removed from service, labeled Out of Service, sent for vendor repair.

title: Skytron 6500 Series - Table Movement Functions Inoperative
description: Troubleshooting guide for Skytron 6500 Series surgical table when movement functions do not operate despite power.

steps.title: Verify Power Source
steps.instructions: Confirm the table is plugged into a live, dedicated outlet. Check for tripped breakers or blown fuses. Even if the table shows partial power (lights), movement motors require full voltage.

steps.title: Check Main Power Switch
steps.instructions: Ensure the main power switch is in the “ON” position. Some Skytron models have separate switches for power and motion enable. Movement functions may be disabled if the motion enable is off.

steps.title: Inspect Hand and Foot Controls
steps.instructions: Ensure all buttons are functioning and not stuck. Verify connection cables are fully seated. Swap with a known good pendant if available. Faulty or disconnected control units often mimic motor failure.

steps.title: Observe Indicator Lights or Error Codes
steps.instructions: Check display panel for error codes or flashing indicators. Document any patterns for repair escalation. Error codes can indicate motor drive or safety interlock issues.

steps.title: Check Safety Interlocks
steps.instructions: Verify table brakes are disengaged and base locks are not activated. Ensure any side rails or attachments are correctly positioned. Many Skytron tables will inhibit movement if safety conditions are not met.

steps.title: Inspect Power Cables and Connectors
steps.instructions: Look for loose or damaged cables, including those connecting the power module to motors. Intermittent or damaged wiring can prevent movement.

steps.title: Test Motion Functions Individually
steps.instructions: Attempt raising/lowering, tilt, and lateral movements one at a time. Note any movement or sounds. Isolates which circuits or motors are affected.

CCR.Complaint: Table powers on but will not move in any direction.
CCR.Cause: All external checks performed; no movement observed from any control; no audible motor activity.
CCR.Resolution: Table removed from service, labeled Out of Service, and sent for repair.

helpfulDetails: Indicator lights normal or error codes documented
No unusual noises, heat, or odor observed
Table removed from service for repair

title: Skytron 6500 Series - Hand Control or Pendant Failure
description: Guide for troubleshooting Skytron 6500 Series surgical table hand control or pendant that does not operate.

steps.title: Visual Inspection
steps.instructions: Check the hand control and pendant for physical damage, loose connectors, or frayed cables. Ensure the connector is fully seated in the table control port. Why: Loose or damaged connections are a frequent cause of control failure.

steps.title: Check Power and Table Status
steps.instructions: Confirm the table powers on and indicator lights on the table base are normal. Verify the table is not in a locked or maintenance mode that disables movement. Why: The hand control may appear nonfunctional if the table itself is not in an active mode.

steps.title: Cable Continuity Test
steps.instructions: If possible, use a multimeter to check the continuity of the pendant cable from the connector to the hand control. Swap the hand control with a known working one, if available. Why: Faulty wiring or internal cable breaks can prevent signal transmission.

steps.title: Battery Check (if applicable)
steps.instructions: Some hand controls use internal batteries. Verify battery voltage and replace if low. Why: Weak or dead batteries can prevent button actuation or lights from functioning.

steps.title: Test Table Response
steps.instructions: With a verified working hand control or pendant, attempt all movement functions. Observe any movement or indicator response. Why: Confirms whether the issue is isolated to the control device or the table electronics.

CCR.Complaint: OR staff report that pressing buttons on the hand control or pendant does not move the table.
CCR.Cause: Visual inspection showed intact cabling; pendant does not respond; table powers on normally.
CCR.Resolution: Hand control/panel swapped with known working unit for verification. Table removed from service and sent for bench evaluation due to likely internal electronics failure.

helpfulDetails: Swapped with known working hand control

title: Skytron 6500 Series – Hydraulic Leaks or Slow Positioning
description: Guide for troubleshooting hydraulic issues on a Skytron 6500 Series surgical table, focusing on slow movement, leaks, and uneven operation.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Verify no patient is on the table. Move any patient to another table if necessary. Confirm table is not under load while troubleshooting.

steps.title: Check Power and Setup
steps.instructions: Confirm table is properly plugged in and power switch is ON. Observe indicator lights to ensure system is active. Power cycling may reset some electronic flow controls.

steps.title: Inspect Hand Control and Pendant
steps.instructions: Verify hand control or foot pendant functions normally. Unresponsive controls can mimic hydraulic slowness. Swap hand control with a known working unit if available.

steps.title: Check Hydraulic Fluid Level (External Reservoir if Accessible)
steps.instructions: Look for fluid sight glass or level indicators. Low fluid can cause slow or uneven movements. Top off only if safe and manufacturer-approved fluid is available.

steps.title: Visual Inspection of Hydraulic Lines and Connections
steps.instructions: Inspect hoses, fittings, and visible cylinders for leaks or damage. Look for wet spots, drips, or fluid stains. Tighten loose fittings carefully without forcing connections.

steps.title: Observe Table Movement Under No Load
steps.instructions: Test each movement (height, tilt, lateral) individually. Note any slow response or irregular motion. Listen for abnormal hissing, groaning, or jerky action.

steps.title: Check for Obstructions
steps.instructions: Ensure base and lift mechanisms are free of debris. Verify cables or other items are not pinched, restricting cylinder travel.

CCR.Complaint: Table moves slowly during height and tilt adjustments; visible hydraulic fluid under base.
CCR.Cause: External inspection revealed minor leaks at a cylinder fitting and low fluid in reservoir.
CCR.Resolution: Table removed from service, labeled Out of Service, and sent for authorized repair.

helpfulDetails: Location of leak and fluid color/type
Final device status: Out of Service

title: Skytron 6500 Series - Table Drift or Position Instability
description: Guide to troubleshoot unintended table drift or inability to maintain position on a Skytron 6500 Series surgical table.

steps.title: Confirm Patient Safety and Table Load
steps.instructions: Ensure no patient is on the table. Remove all accessories or weight plates that may affect movement. Why: Drift can be exaggerated under load; confirming an unloaded table isolates mechanical/hydraulic issues.

steps.title: Check Power and Indicator Lights
steps.instructions: Verify the table is properly plugged in and breaker is on. Confirm main power indicator lights are steady and no error codes are displayed. Why: Inconsistent power can cause control or drift behavior.

steps.title: Inspect Foot and Hand Controls
steps.instructions: Disconnect and reconnect hand and foot pendants. Check for stuck buttons, damage, or moisture. Why: A partially stuck or malfunctioning switch can send unintended movement signals.

steps.title: Check Table Leveling and Casters
steps.instructions: Ensure floor surface is even and casters are locked. Why: Unlevel support can create the appearance of drift when it is mechanical.

steps.title: Inspect Hydraulic Lines and Connections
steps.instructions: Visually inspect under the table for leaks or loose fittings. Look for wet spots, worn hoses, or damaged seals. Why: Hydraulic leaks reduce pressure and allow table segments to drift.

steps.title: Test Individual Table Functions
steps.instructions: Cycle each motion (height, tilt, lateral, Trendelenburg) individually without load. Note if drift occurs in a specific axis or all motions. Why: Isolating functions can indicate if the drift is system-wide or limited to one actuator.

steps.title: Check for Environmental Factors
steps.instructions: Ensure temperature and room conditions are within manufacturer specs. Check for electrical interference or nearby equipment that could affect electronics.

CCR.Complaint: Table drifts when set in Trendelenburg position; cannot maintain height.
CCR.Cause: No external issues found; hand controls and power verified; hydraulic lines visually intact.
CCR.Resolution: Table removed from service, labeled Out of Service, sent for bench evaluation.

helpfulDetails: Verify outlet voltage and breaker condition
Document hand and foot control response
Note any unusual sounds or heat from hydraulic motors
Final device status: Out of Service
